codeReview.summary: The adapter bridge is implemented, but the registry does not satisfy the feature's core acceptance criteria. The mission feature and VAL-ASYNC-002 require hooks of a type to be called in sequence / all registered hooks to run, while the shipped contract stores only one handler per hook type and overwrites earlier registrations. The focused tests reinforce that single-handler behavior instead of catching the gap.

issues.description: The registry stores only one handler per hook type (`mapping(bytes32 => address)`), and `registerHook`/`executeHooks` only replace and invoke that single address. This contradicts `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/features.json:1837-1845` and `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/validation-contract.md:471-473`, which require `executeHooks` to iterate over all registered hooks and call them in sequence. Registering a second hook for the same type silently drops the first one, so the implementation cannot satisfy `executeHooks calls all registered hooks`.

The focused test suite codifies the wrong single-handler semantics via `overwrites an existing handler silently` and never exercises multiple handlers for the same hook type. As written, the tests would still pass even though the acceptance criteria require sequential execution of all registered hooks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This feature mixes contract implementation with live deployment work, but the assigned `solidity-worker` skill explicitly says live testnet deployment should be handed to `deployment-worker`. That mismatch led the worker to defer part of the written feature description as left undone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/features.json:1837` includes `Deploy to testnet with XcmLiquidationNotifier as first registered hook`; `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/solidity-worker/SKILL.md:89` says `Feature requires deployment to live testnet (deployment-worker handles that)`; `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-19T00-32-09-549Z__liquidation-hook-registry__5fd956d5-8fca-40be-954c-764d2bea2485.json:12` records that deployment/wiring was deferred.

summary: Review failed. Commit `40bf44f` adds the adapter and governance-controlled registry plumbing, but the core registry semantics are wrong: it only supports one handler per hook type, so it cannot execute hooks in sequence or call all registered hooks as required by VAL-ASYNC-002. I also recorded one shared-state observation about the feature description assigning live deployment work to the wrong worker skill.
